title: thismia hawkesii
intro: Thismia hawkesii, also known as the "Queen of the Flower Garden," is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the Thismiaceae family. Native to New Zealand, it is a type of subterranean orchid that grows in the dark, producing small white flowers as a lure for female fungus gnats, which are necessary for pollination. The medicinal properties of Thismia hawkesii are not extensively studied, but its roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. The plant's roots can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are said to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and improving digestion.
